

-- 1 of 31 --

16 TYPES OF VIDEO	16 TYPES OF VIDEO
WRITTEN BY KRISTINE MIRELLE
To fill your funnel with paying customers
We all know we should be posting on social media! But sometimes it’s hard to know
where to start or how to come up with new and fresh ideas!
I’m going to share 16 awesome content strategies that I create to bring in leads and
sales to my funnels so that I can grow my business even while I’m sleeping!
I’ll include real life examples, “How to” explanations and more!
Check out the video below and I’ll give you the scoop!

-- 2 of 31 --

TIP #1:	TIP #1: 	CREATE A HOOK!	CREATE A HOOK!
WRITTEN BY KRISTINE MIRELLE
You need a hook that will make your ideal customer keep watching your video! You
could have the best offer in the world, but if you can’t capture their attention in the
first few seconds, they will scroll off, and never even hear about your awesome
product or service.
A 	video 	hook 	is 	a 	technique 	used 	at 	the
beginning 	of 	a 	video 	to 	grab 	the 	viewer's
attention and entice them to keep watching.
Your 	hook 	will 	basically 	be 	the 	biggest
determining factor of how well your video will
perform, how it will sell, if it will go viral, or if it
will 	effectively 	bring 	in 	more 	leads 	and
business for you!
I 	like 	to 	create 	curiosity 	based 	headlines
where I use headlines where I don’t reveal
what the premise of the entire video will be.
Check out the video example!
“Your fans hate when you do this. Do
This instead”
We are going to learn how to apply this in
multiple ways in the next few steps and I’ll be
sharing examples too! Let’s dive in
What is a video hook?

-- 3 of 31 --

TIP #2:	TIP #2: 	MARKET USING INFO-BASED MARKETING	MARKET USING INFO-BASED MARKETING
WRITTEN BY KRISTINE MIRELLE
Info-based marketing is perfect for marketers who hate “selling” or “pitching” people
but also want to be seen as the “authority” or “expert” in their business.
Info 	based 	marketing 	is 	a 	strategy 	where 	you 	share 	valuable 	and 	relevant
information to your audience. The main goal is to offer solutions, tutorials, resources,
and education to help your viewers solve their problems which leads them to trust
your brand and ultimately become one of your customers!
Here are 3 examples of Info-based marketing that I have used in my own company,
and two more examples of work we did for another client.
What is info-based marketing?
76.9K VIEWS 	3,147 LIKES 	144K VIEWS

-- 4 of 31 --

WRITTEN BY KRISTINE MIRELLE
So how do we come up with ideas for information based marketing videos like this?
I’ll share with you a couple ways I do it!
Here’s how! I wrote out an A.I. prompt below. Just customize it and put it into Chat
GPT and it will generate ideas for you!
I want to create 60-second reels for my social media to promote
my [NICHE] business, where I teach [IDEAL CUSTOMER] how to
[DESIRED RESULT].
I'd like my daily reels to address and provide solutions to my
audience's problems and challenges. Can you give me 30 ideas in
the form of questions that I could answer in my video content,
solving one of my customer's problems in each reel?
You can write down the challenges you know your customers have and then
address the solutions in your video.
1.
I can easily come up with 30 days of content by using A.I. to do it for me!	2.
A.I. Prompt #1
I want to create 60-second reels for my social media to promote my [NICHE]
business, where I teach [IDEAL CUSTOMER] how to [DESIRED RESULT].
I'd like my daily reels to address and provide solutions to my audience's problems
and challenges. Can you give me 30 ideas in the form of How To Tutorials, stories I
could tell, or lists like “3 ways to achieve [RESULT]” and other creative forms of content
that would be valuable content to my readers?
A.I. Prompt #2
EXAMPLE
I want to create 60-second reels for my social media to promote my fitness coaching
business, where I teach busy professionals how to stay fit and healthy.
I'd like my daily reels to address and provide solutions to my audience's problems and
challenges. Can you give me 30 ideas in the form of questions that I could answer in my
video content, solving one of my customer's problems in each reel?

-- 5 of 31 --

TIP #3:	TIP #3: 	TELL FUNNY PERSONAL STORIES	TELL FUNNY PERSONAL STORIES
WRITTEN BY KRISTINE MIRELLE
I love to post funny stories from my past that somehow ties into my business. People
LOVE story-telling but you want to tie it into why they might be interested in 	your
products or services.
I also love to talk about the times that I failed because it shows the world that I am
human. I still talk about the times I had success, but in these examples, I share about
the times I struggled, to show people how a normal person can achieve success. It
makes us relatable and makes the viewer go, “well if she can do it despite those
challenges, so can I”.
And guess? Did you know that you know EXACTLY how to create a hook that will
instantly grab someone’s attention and make them want to keep watching? Yep,
YOU ALREADY KNOW HOW.
THAT TIE INTO YOUR BUSINESS
13.8 K VIEWS 	10.4 K VIEWS
Have 	you 	ever 	noticed
that when you’re about to
share 	some 	juicy 	gossip
with a friend or colleague
that 	you 	start 	with 	the
most 	interesting 	thing
about the story FIRST? For
example:
Did you hear about who
Mary is dating now?
Did you know Bobby peed
his pants while on stage?

-- 6 of 31 --

WRITTEN BY KRISTINE MIRELLE
Notice that the titles of these videos mention the most interesting thing in the video
and not only titled it that in the thumbnail, but I also say it first in the video.
First, I tell a story about how Mariah Carey asked me a question one day while I was
playing piano in a restaurant back when I was a full-time musician. You’ll notice a
couple of things. I start out with both a visual hook and a verbal hook. The visual hook
is that the headline is right in the first second, on the thumbnail, and there’s a picture
of Mariah Carey. The headline says “Mariah Carey asked me THIS question”. Take a
special note that I didn’t say what she asked me. I wanted to peak people’s curiosity
and make them watch to find out. At the end, I talk about how despite the #epicfail, I
still went on to succeed.
I then encourage them and give them a call to action to enter into my funnel.
I do the same thing in the story “I peed my pants”. I talk about a time I totally failed
on stage, but then talk about how I went on to perform 2500 paid shows around the
world. I then let people know
In the next example on the previous page, its about one of the first times I was ever
on stage where I peed in my pants in front of hundreds of people and they had to
drag me off stage. A puddle of pee remained on the stage. At the end I go on to
mention how I’ve now performed 2500 shows, big and small, around the world. My
main message is that if someone who once peed on stage can go on to making a
living out of being on stage, imagine what you can do!
I then encourage them and give them a call to action to enter into my funnel.
For you, think about any funny, interesting, or shocking stories from your past. Then
think of how you can tie into how your current business offers a solution to your
customer’s challenges.

-- 7 of 31 --

FOR EXAMPLE:
WRITTEN BY KRISTINE MIRELLE
TIP #4:	TIP #4: 	SHARING HOW OTHER PEOPLE	SHARING HOW OTHER PEOPLE
I love sharing success stories to help inspire and motivate my audience. In Tip #2, I
talked about creating 'How To' tutorials. Another great idea is to make tutorials or
show case studied that show how others have achieved their goals.
ARE GETTING RESULTS
This group Is getting $2500 a show 18.6K Views
She lost 85 pounds in 90 days and still ate cake
every day. Here’s how
He found the love of her life at 50 years old on
THIS website in only 4 weeks
How this retired grandma earned 6 figures in
one month in only 1 hour a day
Here’s an example that I created for my audience:
In this example I share the story of a group that I worked with personally so it made a
great example of results someone could achieve by using my products whom I’ve
actually worked with personally.
Alternatively, you could use someone else’s achievements as a great video to share
even if you haven’t worked with them for example.
Let’s say you teach moms how to start their own online E-Commerce business even
while raising children without sacrificing family time. You could share a case study of
a mom who built her Ecom business, how it changed her life and her family, and then
you can offer your own lead magnet, mini course, or whatever it is that you’re
promoting.

-- 8 of 31 --

THIS IS WHY YOU’RE A BROKE MUSICIAN
WRITTEN BY KRISTINE MIRELLE
TIP #5:	TIP #5: 	CALLING PEOPLE OUT:	CALLING PEOPLE OUT:
One of my best performing organic reels in my music information-based business
I’ve ever posted was one where I said “This is why you’re a broke musician”. It’s now
reached more than a million views 	ORGANICALLY and other people have posted it
on THEIR channels and even received hundreds of thousands of plays because of it.
(1 MILLION VIEWS ON THIS ONE VIDEO)
Instead of saying “this is how you make money as a
musician” I decided to go the opposite direction
and make the hook “This is why you’re a broke
musician”. I knew it would trigger people. Some
people 	loved 	it, 	some 	people 	hated 	it, 	and 	it
sparked tons of engagement, got tons of shares,
and reposts by other influencers.
The concept of this strategy is to figure out what
their challenge is, and then put it in a way that
might trigger an emotional response and curiosity.
(BTW I’ve sold more than 40,000 books now in this
particular business!)

-- 9 of 31 --

WRITTEN BY KRISTINE MIRELLE
TIP #6:	TIP #6: 	PODCAST OR LIVE TRAINING STYLE:	PODCAST OR LIVE TRAINING STYLE:
Easiest Way to Create Content
One very simple style of creating video content is to shoot it “Podcast style”. This is
where you can look away from the camera “interview-style”, and create valuable
content for your audience that typically will feel more conversational and even more
entertaining because it appears to be on a podcast.
Option 1: You can actually film yourself on a podcast. (I will cover that in a different
section) But let’s say you have no upcoming podcasts schedule? Then try this next
tip!
EASIEST OPTION: Rather than having to go book yourself on actual podcasts and
hope you get some golden nuggets in there, I love to shoot valuable content and
just pretend. It makes the content on your social media more varied and not the
same thing over and over again. The main important thing is that we are delivering
solutions to our audience’s problems. Whether you’re looking at the camera, looking
away from the camera, reading from a book, a teleprompter, or hanging upside
down from a tree, our main objective is to help them with their challenges. The
more creative we can be in doing so, the more effective we can be at helping them.
The more we help our customers, the more value we bring. The more value we
bring, the more monetary value we can collect.
In this example I actually created a reel on how to
create reels. LOL. This got hundreds of thousands of
views and shows you how I do it!
On some examples below, I opened my computer and
talked directly to it as though I was on a live training.
Again, our only goal is to share valuable information to
our audience so no matter how we deliver it, we are
helping 	to 	change 	the 	world 	and 	influence 	people
along the way! This is just one fun way to do it and my
personal favorite.
292K Views 6,197 Likes. 4,460 Shares

-- 10 of 31 --

“LIVE TRAINING STYLE”
WRITTEN BY KRISTINE MIRELLE
How to get on radio 98.6k
Views 4,400 likes 2,331 Shares
Best tool to schedule social
media content 25.9K Views 547
Shares 973 likes

media content 25.9K Views 547
Shares 973 likes

media content 25.9K Views 547
Shares 973 likes

-- 11 of 31 --

WRITTEN BY KRISTINE MIRELLE
I created the A.I. prompt below to help you! It will generate 30 questions for you and
when you start recording your content, you can look into your computer screen and
answer each question as though you are talking directly to someone. The goal is to
be as natural as possible.
I want to create 60-second reels for my social media to
promote 	my 	[NICHE] 	business, 	where 	I 	teach 	[IDEAL
CUSTOMER] how to achieve [DESIRED RESULT].
In these reels, I'll be pretending to talk into my computer as
if 	I'm 	on 	a 	podcast 	or 	live 	training 	session. 	Could 	you
provide me with 30 questions that my audience might
commonly 	ask? 	These 	should 	address 	pain 	points 	or
objections they currently face and would find valuable if
answered."
A.I. Prompt

-- 12 of 31 --

WRITTEN BY KRISTINE MIRELLE
TIP #7:	TIP #7: 	SOCIAL PROOF:	SOCIAL PROOF:
If you are a coach who has achieved the thing you are teaching, it’s great for you to
show those moments on social media. It gives you credibility since they discover that
they are learning from someone who has actually done the thing they are trying to
learn how to do.
In this next example, I combine two things. 1. I combine information-marketing, because
I share 	an example of a “speaker reel”. This is for my audience of entrepreneurs who
speak from stage and have info products to sell.
2nd. It positions me as someone they can trust because I’ve actually done the thing
that I am teaching!
In this example I didn’t get nearly as many views as my other
videos but I received a lot of value. I had many people sign up
for my email list AND a entrepreneurial conference reached
out and asked me if I would speak to their live audience as
well.
Side note: When I speak at live events, I usually get at least a
couple people who buy into my high ticket products so this
definitely means this post created a high amount of income
for me considering that speaking at the conference will likely
bring in a buyers of some of my coaching or “done for you”
programs that cost upwards of $25K.
This 	is 	a 	DOUBLE 	WIN 	for 	my 	coaching 	business 	for
entrepreneurs.
In my coaching business where we coach specifically just
musicians, I created a performance “show off” piece. (On next
page) I shared this particular video of me playing a piano
piece that I wrote to my music community simply just to say
“look what I can do!” It shows that I am an expert at what I do,
but more on the creative side.

-- 13 of 31 --

WRITTEN BY KRISTINE MIRELLE
Playing an original piano piece
For you, let’s say you teach people yoga. You might
share a video of you doing an advanced Yoga position.
If you are a martial arts instructor, you might show off
doing some difficult moves that only someone who
has been doing this for decades would be able to
master. If you teach how to invest in real estate, you
might post a video of you standing in front of a block of
real estate that you are currently building or have
already built.
Think about how you can show off your “work” and
build trust with your community by showing what you
can do in the field that you are teaching.
More Social Proof: Business Conference Funnel
Hacking Live:
This is footage from when I spoke at Funnel Hacking
Live 2021. By sharing this footage it gives me credibility
and when I share that I will be teaching a course on
exactly what I shared on stage but in depth, people
definitely get excited!
This was a big business conference that I spoke at so
selling anything that has to do with growing a business
would 	be 	a 	perfect 	addition 	here! 	If 	you 	are 	a
nutritionist 	and 	you 	spoke 	at 	a 	conference 	about
nutrition, post that! If you presented at a college, or
even a small business meeting at work, any and all of
this makes great video content!
Tie it back to your products and services by showing yourself doing the thing that builds
credibility with your audience. For example, if the video shows you talking on stage at a
fitness conference about how to eat to live to be 100, then you could play off of that and
say “I will be covering more about how to eat to live to 100 in my up coming challenge! I
only got to speak for 30 minutes to this group, but we will be covering in depth for five
whole days!” See how this positions your event as super exclusive?

-- 14 of 31 --

WRITTEN BY KRISTINE MIRELLE
Performing in front of 10,000 people
CELL PHONE VIDEOS
Don’t underestimate the power of posting a good cell
phone video! Often, simple videos of me on my cell
phone 	outperform 	even 	professional 	videos 	that 	I
invest money and time into! They can get more views,
sales, and even more reach outs from potential clients.
We look more human! We are more approachable and
social media is all about connecting with people. I
often post videos like this and get a great response!
These two videos are more examples of me “doing the
thing” that I help others achieve. One of my businesses
focuses entirely on musicians so the more I show them
that I am also a musician

-- 15 of 31 --

WRITTEN BY KRISTINE MIRELLE
TIP #8:	TIP #8: 	EDUCATIONAL SKITS:	EDUCATIONAL SKITS:
CLICK TO WATCH THIS SKIT >>>>Raising Prices
Fun skits are a hilarious way to bond with your
customer. 	These 	also 	have 	a 	high 	chance 	of
getting shared, viewers tagging their friends, and
getting tons of likes and comments.
In 	these 	examples: 	My 	target 	customers 	are
coaches. I focus on emphasizing the pain points of
the 	customer. 	For 	many 	business 	owners, 	they
struggle when it comes to talking about the price of
their products and services. I EXAGGERATED that
pain point to the point that it’s ridiculous and funny.
You’ll get brownie points from your audience for
being fun!
In the second skit, I cover how to create a value
stack which is a concept entrepreneurs use to add
more 	value 	to 	what 	they 	are 	selling! 	This 	is
educational but also fun at the same time.
Another awesome reason to have skits, is you can
use these in your webinars or presentations on
stage. I can teach something to my audience, and
then play the skit, and it always gets the crowd
laughing and more entertained and engaged.
This is specifically a “Expert Vs. Beginner” type skit
where the “beginner” covers common objections of
the 	customer 	and 	the 	expert 	is 	able 	to 	handle
those objections in a fun and entertaining way!

-- 16 of 31 --

WRITTEN BY KRISTINE MIRELLE
TIP #8:	TIP #8: 	SNIPPETS FROM YOUR TRAININGS	SNIPPETS FROM YOUR TRAININGS
Training on Spotify “Spotify Sucks”
If you do 1 on 1 coaching, webinars, if you hold
challenges, masterclasses, or any type of online
training, 	posting 	valuable 	snippets 	from 	your
videos REALLY position you as an expert in your
business. 	They 	view 	you 	as 	an 	expert 	and 	the
bonus is that you’re posting valuable content so
they also get the value of whatever you’re sharing.
But what if you don’t have time to go edit your
video and go through your trainings every time?
I have an A.I. tool that I LOVE to use. You literally just
paste the video into this app, and it will seek out the
valuable topics you cover, edit it for you into short
form 	content, 	give 	it 	a 	headline, 	format 	it 	for
whatever social media platform you wish, AND ADD
CAPTIONS. (Crazy right?)
This was a very simple clip taken from a zoom
recording that I had done.
OR PODCASTS
CLICK HERE 	TO TRY OUT VIDYO.AI

-- 17 of 31 --

WRITTEN BY KRISTINE MIRELLE
TIP #9:	TIP #9: 	DANCING OR DOING SOMETHING	DANCING OR DOING SOMETHING
That feeling you get when you start earning
from your music 9,654 Views
This is a very simple strategy and is SUPER fast to
create. Simply show yourself doing something fun
in the video. 	Dancing, jumping up and down, or
doing anything where you’re celebrating is great!
Then you can put text on the screen that says
things like “That feeling you get when you finally
lose your first ten pounds in 2 weeks and still ate
brownies!” You’re basically highlighting a result that
you or your students have gotten and how great it
eels!
Here are a few other examples as well!
Niche: Real Estate Coach
Dancing in front of a new home with a sign in front
Text over the video: “When you celebrate your first home purchase without having to
spend any of your own money”
Cryptocurrency Investment Coach
Video 2: Driving up in your dream car, stepping out of the driver’s side looking super
cool
Text over the video: When you drive up in your dream car with money you made
from investing in crypto
Example 3: Fitness Coach
Video: Trying on super cute clothing
Text over the video: When you fit into your old clothes because you lost all your
pregnancy weight
VISUALLY ENTERTAINING WITH RESULT BASED
TEXT OVERLAY

-- 18 of 31 --

WRITTEN BY KRISTINE MIRELLE
TIP #10:	TIP #10: 	TEXT OVER VIDEO	TEXT OVER VIDEO
We’ve covered how you can offer
“information based marketing”
earlier in this book. Another great
alternative is to offer great
information to your audience but
in the form of text over video. Here
are some other examples:
6 foods you should never eat if
you’re trying to lose weight
5 software programs I used to
build 7 figure business
3 books that saved my
marriage
Pro Tip: In this example, I let them know I’ll send them a discount code to my favorite
app. I establish affiliate relationships with the apps that I promote, I request a
discount affiliate code I can offer to my audience, and then I send out the code to
anyone who comments on my post. Then I receive affiliate commissions when they
sign up to use their apps. I only promote services I actually use and can vouch that
they work and are awesome.
(INFORMATION BASED CONTENT)

-- 19 of 31 --

“This or that” questions are awesome because it gets tons of
engagement! 	This 	type 	of 	video 	content 	should 	still 	be
relatable to your ideal customer.
Let’s 	say 	you 	are 	a 	marriage 	coach, 	you 	wouldn’t 	ask
something like “Coke or Pepsi?” Instead you might say, “Best
date night idea with your husband? Netflix and chill? Or Club
dancing?”
I created an A.I. prompt that you could easily customize and
then pop into Chat GPT to give you tons of ideas for “This or
That” questions that still bring in your ideal customer!
Then you can add a Call to action like “comment the word
‘date’ and I’ll send you 48 fun and exciting date night ideas
for married couples”.
WRITTEN BY KRISTINE MIRELLE
TIP #11:	TIP #11: 	TEXT OVER VIDEO THIS OR THAT QUESTIONS	TEXT OVER VIDEO THIS OR THAT QUESTIONS
Examples include:
[INSERT EXAMPLE]
Example customized prompt:
I want to create content for social media to promote my [NICHE] business, where I
teach [IDEAL CUSTOMER] how to [DESIRED RESULT].
Can you give me 30 ideas in the form of “This or that” questions” or ‘Would you rather”
type questions that would create engagement while also attracting my ideal customer
that I could post on my social media accounts?
I want to create content for social media to promote my [NICHE]
business, where I teach [IDEAL CUSTOMER] how to [DESIRED RESULT].
Can you give me 30 ideas in the form of “This or that” questions” or
‘Would you rather” type questions that would create engagement
while also attracting my ideal customer that I could post on my
social media accounts?
A.I. Prompt

-- 20 of 31 --

WRITTEN BY KRISTINE MIRELLE
TIP #12:	TIP #12: 	TESTIMONIALS SCREEN SHOTS	TESTIMONIALS SCREEN SHOTS
One of my favorite types of social media that brings in really great leads and sales
is by doing two simple things.
Film yourself pointing down or up, show yourself dancing, celebrating, jumping
up and down or anything that would bring attention to the text on the screen.
Take 	a 	screen 	shot 	of 	a 	testimonial 	that 	one 	of 	your 	students 	or 	happy
customers have said about their experience with you.
I often film 5 videos of me pointing or dancing and then interchange testimonials
every now and then so that I don’t have to keep filming new videos for these types
of posts!
Student books their first gig
with popular song
Guy books first $10,000 gig 	Artist books their first 6
months contract on a cruise
ship

-- 21 of 31 --

WRITTEN BY KRISTINE MIRELLE
TIP #13:	TIP #13: 	VIDEO TESTIMONIALS FROM	VIDEO TESTIMONIALS FROM
Showing testimonials from your customers is a way to help your future customers
build trust with you. They get to see other people of varying races, genders, ages,
and other demographics that help them to begin to see themselves in that person
too. “If that person can do it, I can do it too”.
To get these testimonials I often ask my customers to send over what their results
have been in a video format, or I may personally ask someone if I can interview
them so I can ask them questions that I can use a testimonial. Questions like “what
was your biggest challenge” “what was your favorite thing about working together”
and “What were you most excited about”, can get you some epic footage you can
post on your page! I noticed these don’t get a huge amount of views, but I do get a
lot of direct messages in my inbox when I post these and that’s the only data that
matters! 	Our 	goal 	is 	to 	get 	more 	paying 	customers 	and 	leads 	and 	video
testimonials are a killer way to do that.
Below are a couple examples.
Boy group makes $2500 a
show
Student books their first show
at a festival
3RD EXAMPLE- Tom camp
makes $30K in one week
HAPPY CUSTOMERS

-- 22 of 31 --

WRITTEN BY KRISTINE MIRELLE
TIP #14:	TIP #14: 	SCREEN SHOTS OF RESULTS,	SCREEN SHOTS OF RESULTS,
CHARTS, DATA	CHARTS, DATA
Another great way to show proof of concept and build trust with your customers is
to screen shot results that are measurable to your ideal customer.
In my examples below, I share financial screen shots of accounts showing a specific
result that myself of my students have accomplished. I teach mostly things around
making money so this is a great addition to my social media content. You can
screen shot pics of bank accounts, a check, a new wire that came in, etc.
Here are a few other examples by niche:
Health coach: Share photo of someone’s blood pressure reading finally back to
normal
College Tutoring Software: Photo of someone’s report card before and after
using the software
Real estate coach: Photo of someone’s approval for their mortgage loan
$16,000 in sales in my subscription program
Stripe account of Cologne sales
Stripe account of Cologne sales

-- 23 of 31 --

WRITTEN BY KRISTINE MIRELLE
TIP #15:	TIP #15: 	TRENDING SOUNDS	TRENDING SOUNDS
There is no way (Song)
When I see a funny trending sound that people are
using, I ask myself if there’s a way I can use it for my
business. In the example “There is no way”, I had
seen someone use this on a real estate profile. I then
repurposed 	it 	for 	my 	music 	community. 	Then 	I
repurposed 	it 	for 	my 	business 	where 	I 	teach
entrepreneurs 	how 	to 	build 	and 	scale 	online
businesses. So I used it for two completely different
businesses, 	with 	same 	exact 	video 	and 	trending
sound.
For “You sure about that” I asked myself again how
this would fit into my music information businesses
where 	I 	teach 	musicians 	how 	to 	build 	their
independent music careers.
You sure about that?
How can you easily find trending audio? Here are a
couple great ways:
Tip 1: Scroll through your social media feed and when
you find a sound you want to use, make sure to save
it!
Tip 	2: 	Visit 	TRENDING 	PAGE 	ON 	TIKTOK 	This 	has
different features and filters so you can find trending
audio
Tip 	3: 	This 	account 	shares 	trending 	sounds 	on
Instagram 	once 	a 	week 	and 	was 	created 	by
Instagram specifically to help creators stay up to
date with current video trends.

-- 24 of 31 --

I posted a video of me speaking to the camera that has hit around 1 million views
now organically. A few months later someone else reposted the video, made
absolutely no changes to it, and received 140,000 views to the video on their page.
They got likes, shares, and TONS of new followers. They are also in the music
business, and since the content I share is on the topic of music business, they
were able to attract their ideal customer. I thought it was genius.
Then, just a few days later, someone else reposted it and then reacted to it in the
video by adding their own commentary.
For you as a content creator, You can find other valuable similar influencer’s
content, and then create a reaction video. What’s great about this is it attracts
your ideal customer but leveraging other people’s content. This keeps your
content fresh and new.
I also like this because if you give an awesome shout out to them in the caption,
this could be a great way to create an affiliate relationship, or just a relationship
in general that could possibly lead to collaborations in the future.
WRITTEN BY KRISTINE MIRELLE
TIP #16:	TIP #16: 	REACTION TO SOMEONE ELSE’S	REACTION TO SOMEONE ELSE’S
EXAMPLE 1 This is the guy
who reposted my content
that got hundreds of
thousands of views
EXAMPLE 2 This guy
reposted my content
with a reaction to it
EXAMPLE 3 This is an example
of one of my friends who posts
reaction videos in the finance
space
VIRAL VIDEO

-- 25 of 31 --

TIP #17:	TIP #17: 	ENCOURAGING STATS TO	ENCOURAGING STATS TO
One big objection that my music community has about pursuing their music
career is that they’re too old. So I created a video that shows proof that you can
have success later in life and also included the names of the popular artists that
have actually achieved major success later in life.
I then repeated this same concept but for “entrepreneurs who made it later in life.
For you, list out what objections someone might have as to why they can’t use
your product or service, and then share stories of how other people have found
success despite their challenges.
Business: Dating Coach
Objection: “I can’t find a husband. No one will want a woman with kids”
Your video: “Women with children who found the love of their life”
Business: Fitness Coach
*Objection: "I’m too busy to find time for exercise."
Your video:"Busy Professionals Who Transformed Their Bodies only 45 minutes a
day.
My Example:
Niche: Musicians who want to have careers
Objection: I am too old to make it in music
Video: Artists who made it later in life
WRITTEN BY KRISTINE MIRELLE
OVERCOME OBJECTIONS

-- 26 of 31 --

WRITTEN BY KRISTINE MIRELLE
TIP #18:	TIP #18: 	USE AN A.I. VIRTUAL ASSISTANT	USE AN A.I. VIRTUAL ASSISTANT
In all of your video content, 	it’s always important to have a call to action. This
means you are driving your viewer to take an action. Every business is different so
that might be to join an email list, buy a product, set up a consultation call, etc.
This is how it works. Let’s say I’m sharing a tutorial on how to build a low-ticket-
funnel that drives leads 24 hours of the day. At the end of the video I can say,
“would you like to learn how I built multiple low ticket funnels that have generated
7 figures each even though I was a busy mom? Comment “mom money” and I’ll
send you a link to learn more”. When someone types “mom money” in the
comment, I can set my auto responder to automatically DM them a link to my
“Cash flow automation” mini-course where I teach exactly how to do that.
It's like having a virtual assistant that replies to everyone for you and can help you
get more leads and sales even while you’re sleeping!
There are many auto responder tools out there including Many Chat, Instachamp,
and more. If you use my affiliate link below you’ll get a discount while trying out
instachamp!
TO MARKET FOR YOU
INSTACHAMP
SUBMAGIC
TIP #19:	TIP #19: 	ADDING IMAGES/ANIMATIONS	ADDING IMAGES/ANIMATIONS
If you’ve noticed that my videos have fun animations and B-roll on many of them,
you may be asking what our team uses to edit these! We use an app called
Submagic and it uses A.I. to actually add everything for you! Check it out below
